question_paraphrased: Do you feel like the Democratic party actually stands up for people in your situation? (Options: Very well, Somewhat well, Not too well, Not at all well, Refused)
implicit_questions: When you look at the current legislative priorities of Democratic leadership, how often do you see your own personal needs being championed?

entries.input_attribute: I feel that if someone has different political views, it usually means our broader values and goals are also quite different.
entries.original_question: They may feel differently than I do about politics
entries.subject: feel differently than I do about politics
entries.prompt: They may {} (Options: But they probably share many of my other values and goals, And they probably don't share many of my other values and goals, either, Refused)
entries.target: And they probably don't share many of my other values and goals, either
question_paraphrased: Even if someone has different political views than you, do you think you still share common ground in other areas of life? (Options: But they probably share many of my other values and goals, And they probably don't share many of my other values and goals, either, Refused)
implicit_questions: To what extent do you find that political disagreements with others tend to reflect a deeper divide in how you both view right and wrong?
How do you generally feel about assessing the personal values of others based on their political affiliations?
When you talk to people with different political leanings, how often do you find yourselves agreeing on basic life principles like family, community, or work ethics?
